author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm XVII

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A prayer of David himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This prayer must be assigned to the Person of the Lord, with the addition of the Church, which is His body.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Hear My righteousness, O God, consider My supplication” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Hearken unto My prayer, not in deceitful lips:” not going forth to Thee in deceitful lips.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let My judgment from Thy countenance go forth” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From the enlightening of the knowledge of Thee, let Me judge truth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Or at least, let My judgment go forth, not in deceitful lips, from Thy countenance, that is, that I may not in judging utter aught else than I understand in Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let Mine eyes see equity:” the eyes, of course, of the heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast proved and visited Mine heart in the night-season” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this Mine heart hath been proved by the visitation of tribulation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast examined Me by fire, and iniquity hath not been found in Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now not night only, in that it is wont to disturb, but fire also, in that it burns, is this tribulation to be called; whereby when I was examined I was found righteous.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “That My mouth may not speak the works of men” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That nothing may proceed out of My mouth, but what relates to Thy glory and praise; not to the works of men, which they do beside Thy will.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Because of the words of Thy lips.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because of the words of Thy peace, or of Thy prophets.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have kept hard ways.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I have kept the toilsome ways of human mortality and suffering.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “To perfect My steps in Thy paths” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That the love of the Church might be perfected in the strait ways, whereby she arrives at Thy rest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “That My footsteps be not moved.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That the signs of My way, which, like footsteps, have been imprinted on the Sacraments and Apostolical writings, be not moved, that they may mark them who would follow Me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Or at least, that I may still abide fixedly in eternity, after that I have accomplished the hard ways, and have finished My steps in the straits of Thy paths.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have cried out, for Thou hast heard Me, O God” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With a free and strong effort have I directed My prayers unto Thee: for that I might have this power, Thou hast heard Me when praying more weakly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Incline Thine ear to Me, and hear My words.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let not Thy hearing forsake My humiliation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Make Thy mercies marvellous” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let not Thy mercies be disesteemed, lest they be loved too little.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who savest them that hope in Thee from such as resist Thy right hand:” from such as resist the favour, whereby Thou favourest Me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Keep Me, O Lord, as the apple of Thine eye” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 8): which seems very little and minute: yet by it is the sight of the eye directed, whereby the light is distinguished from the darkness; as by Christ’s humanity, the divinity of the Judgment distinguishing between the righteous and sinners.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In the covering of Thy wings protect Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the defence of Thy love and mercy protect Me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “From the face of the ungodly who have troubled Me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Mine enemies have compassed about My soul;” “they have shut up their own fat” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They have been covered with their own gross joy, after that their desire hath been satiated with wickedness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Their mouth hath spoken pride.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And therefore their mouth spoke pride, in saying, “Hail, King of the Jews,” and other like words.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Casting Me forth they have now compassed Me about” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Casting Me forth outside the city, they have now compassed Me about on the Cross.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Their eyes they have determined to turn down on the earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The bent of their heart they have determined to turn down on these earthly things: deeming Him, who was slain, to endure a mighty evil, and themselves, that slew Him, none.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “As a lion ready for prey, have they taken Me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They have taken Me, like that adversary who “walketh about, seeking whom he may devour.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And as a lion’s whelp dwelling in secret places.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And as his whelp, the people to whom it was said, “Ye are of your father the devil:” meditating on the snares, whereby they might circumvent and destroy the just One.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Arise, O Lord, prevent them, and cast them down” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Arise, O Lord, Thou whom they suppose to be asleep, and regardless of men’s iniquities; be they blinded before by their own malice, that vengeance may prevent their deed; and so cast them down.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Deliver My soul from the ungodly.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Deliver My soul, by restoring Me after the death, which the ungodly have inflicted on Me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thy weapon: from the enemies of Thine hand” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For My soul is Thy weapon, which Thy hand, that is, Thy eternal Power, hath taken to subdue thereby the kingdoms of iniquity, and divide the righteous from the ungodly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This weapon then “deliver from the enemies of Thine hand,” that is, of Thy Power, that is, from Mine enemies.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Destroy them, O Lord, from off the earth, scatter them in their life.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O Lord, destroy them from off the earth, which they inhabit, scatter them throughout the world in this life, which only they think their life, who despair of life eternal.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And by Thy hidden things their belly hath been filled.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now not only this visible punishment shall overtake them, but also their memory hath been filled with sins, which as darkness are hidden from the light of Thy truth, that they should forget God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They have been filled with swine’s flesh.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They have been filled with uncleanness, treading under foot the pearls of God’s words.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And they have left the rest to their babes:” crying out, “This sin be upon us and upon our children.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But I shall appear in Thy righteousness in Thy sight” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But I, Who have not appeared to them that, with their filthy and darkened heart, cannot see the light of wisdom, “I shall appear in Thy righteousness in Thy sight.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I shall be satiated, when Thy glory shall be manifested.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And when they have been satiated with their uncleanness, that they could not know Me, I shall be satiated, when Thy glory shall be manifested, in them that know Me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In that verse indeed where it is said, “filled with swine’s flesh,” some copies have, “filled with children:” for from the ambiguity of the Greek a double interpretation has resulted.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now by “children” we understand works; and as by good children, good works, so by evil, evil.
